User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the CognitoLearn platform, either a student or a teacher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the platform. Accepted values are 'Student' or 'Teacher'.

profilePictureUrl.description: Optional URL to the user's profile picture.

Class.title: Class
Class.description: Represents an educational class created and managed by a teacher, which students can join.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Class entity.

name.description: The name of the class (e.g., 'Algebra I - Section A').

subject.description: The academic subject of the class (e.g., 'Mathematics').

description.description: A brief description of the class.

teacherId.description: Reference to the User who is the teacher of this class. (Relationship: User 1:N Class)

studentIds.description: References to Users who are students in this class. (Relationship: Class N:N User)

schedule.description: A description of the class schedule (e.g., 'Mon, Wed, Fri 10:00 AM - 11:30 AM').

creationDate.description: The date and time when the class was created.

Note.title: Note
Note.description: Represents an educational note or document uploaded by a teacher or generated by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Note entity.

contentUrl.description: The URL to the actual content of the note (e.g., a PDF or Markdown file in storage).

classId.description: Reference to the Class this note belongs to. (Relationship: Class 1:N Note)

teacherId.description: Reference to the User (teacher) who uploaded or created this note. (Relationship: User 1:N Note)

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the note was uploaded or created.

isAIGenerated.description: Indicates if the note content was generated by AI.

topics.description: Keywords or topics covered in the note, used for recommendations and organization.

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents an educational video uploaded by a teacher or suggested by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity.

videoUrl.description: The URL to the video content (e.g., YouTube link, Firebase Storage URL).

classId.description: Reference to the Class this video belongs to. (Relationship: Class 1:N Video)

teacherId.description: Reference to the User (teacher) who uploaded or created this video. (Relationship: User 1:N Video)

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the video was uploaded or created.

durationMinutes.description: The duration of the video in minutes.

topics.description: Keywords or topics covered in the video, used for recommendations and organization.

isAIGeneratedSuggestion.description: Indicates if this video was suggested by AI.

Quiz.title: Quiz
Quiz.description: Represents an educational quiz created by a teacher or generated by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quiz entity.

description.description: A brief description of the quiz.

classId.description: Reference to the Class this quiz belongs to. (Relationship: Class 1:N Quiz)

teacherId.description: Reference to the User (teacher) who created this quiz. (Relationship: User 1:N Quiz)

creationDate.description: The date and time when the quiz was created.

maxScore.description: The maximum possible score for this quiz.

isAIGenerated.description: Indicates if the quiz content and questions were generated by AI.

topics.description: Keywords or topics covered in the quiz.

quizContentUrl.description: URL to the detailed quiz questions and answer structure, if complex.

QuizAttempt.title: QuizAttempt
QuizAttempt.description: Records a student's attempt at a specific quiz, including their score and submission details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuizAttempt entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the User (student) who took the quiz. (Relationship: User 1:N QuizAttempt)

quizId.description: Reference to the Quiz that was attempted. (Relationship: Quiz 1:N QuizAttempt)

score.description: The score achieved by the student in this attempt.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the quiz was submitted.

feedback.description: AI-generated or teacher-provided feedback on the quiz attempt.

answersJsonUrl.description: Optional URL to a JSON file detailing the student's answers for analysis.

ChatSession.title: ChatSession
ChatSession.description: Represents a private messaging conversation between a teacher and a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatSession entity.

participantIds.description: References to the two Users participating in this chat session (one teacher, one student).

lastMessageTimestamp.description: The timestamp of the last message sent in this session.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents an individual message within a chat session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

chatSessionId.description: Reference to the ChatSession this message belongs to. (Relationship: ChatSession 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the User who sent this message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

content.description: The text content of the message.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the message was sent.

isRead.description: Indicates if the message has been read by the recipient.

StudyGroup.title: StudyGroup
StudyGroup.description: Represents a study group formed within a class, often based on AI suggestions for student performance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudyGroup entity.

classId.description: Reference to the Class this study group belongs to. (Relationship: Class 1:N StudyGroup)

name.description: The name or identifier of the study group.

studentIds.description: References to Users (students) who are members of this study group. (Relationship: StudyGroup N:N User)

teacherId.description: Optional reference to the User (teacher) overseeing this study group. (Relationship: User 1:N StudyGroup, if assigned)

formationDate.description: The date and time when the study group was formed.

focusTopics.description: Specific topics or weak areas this study group is designed to address.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles including their role (Student/Teacher). The 'userId' in the path directly links to 'request.auth.uid' for easy ownership-based security rules. The 'role' field is used for database-backed access control (DBAC).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores educational class details. Includes 'teacherId' for class ownership and a 'studentIds' array for membership, enabling direct authorization checks on the document without 'get()'.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for the class.

definition.description: Notes associated with a specific class. Documents include denormalized 'classTeacherId' (from parent Class's teacherId) and 'classStudentIds' (from parent Class's studentIds) for authorization independence, allowing QAPs.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent class.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the note.

definition.description: Videos associated with a specific class. Documents include denormalized 'classTeacherId' (from parent Class's teacherId) and 'classStudentIds' (from parent Class's studentIds) for authorization independence, allowing QAPs.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent class.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier for the video.

definition.description: Quizzes associated with a specific class. Documents include denormalized 'classTeacherId' (from parent Class's teacherId) and 'classStudentIds' (from parent Class's studentIds) for authorization independence, allowing QAPs.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent class.

params.name: quizId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quiz.

definition.description: Study groups within a class. Documents include 'studentIds' (members of the group) and 'teacherId' (overseeing teacher). Also includes denormalized 'classTeacherId' and 'classStudentIds' (from the parent Class) for robust authorization and QAPs within the class context.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent class.

params.name: studyGroupId
params.description: The unique identifier for the study group.

definition.description: Records a student's attempt at a quiz. The path establishes student ownership. Documents include denormalized 'classId' and 'teacherId' (from the associated Quiz/Class) to enable teachers to query attempts for their classes using collection group queries without 'get()'.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the student who made the attempt.

params.name: quizAttemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quiz attempt.

definition.description: Represents a private messaging conversation. The 'participantIds' array within the document allows direct authorization checks for participants.

params.name: chatSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the chat session.

definition.description: Individual messages within a chat session. Documents include denormalized 'chatSessionParticipantIds' (from the parent ChatSession's participantIds) for authorization independence.

params.name: chatSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent chat session.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for CognitoLearn prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Query-as-Permissions (QAPs). 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
This is achieved by strategically denormalizing authorization-relevant fields from parent documents into their subcollections, or into related documents where cross-collection access is required. For instance, all content within a class (Notes, Videos, Quizzes, StudyGroups) will have the `classTeacherId` and `classStudentIds` (denormalized from the parent Class document) stored directly within each content document. This eliminates the need for expensive and insecure `get()` operations in security rules to check parent document permissions. Similarly, `QuizAttempt` documents, while primarily owned by a student, will denormalize the `classId` and `teacherId` from the associated quiz/class to enable teachers to securely query all attempts for their classes without relying on `get()` calls.

**Support for Query-as-Permissions (QAPs):**
1.  **Homogeneous Collections:** Each collection and subcollection (`/users`, `/classes`, `/classes/{classId}/notes`, etc.) is designed to hold documents with a homogeneous security posture. This simplifies security rules immensely, as a rule applied to a collection can assume consistent access patterns for all its documents.
2.  **Explicit Authorization Fields:** Denormalized fields like `classStudentIds`, `classTeacherId`, and `chatSessionParticipantIds` are directly queryable. For example, a student can query for their assigned notes using `db.collectionGroup('notes').where('classStudentIds', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid)`, and the security rules can enforce this query based on the `classStudentIds` field within the `Note` document itself. This ensures that only authorized data is returned, fulfilling the QAP principle.
3.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For private user data, the `/users/{userId}/...` structure directly embeds the owner's UID in the path, making it trivially easy to enforce ownership rules (`request.auth.uid == userId`).
4.  **Membership Maps:** For collaborative entities like `Classes`, `ChatSessions`, and `StudyGroups`, explicit `participantIds` or `studentIds`/`teacherId` fields (acting as a membership map) are used directly in the document, allowing efficient `array-contains` or direct equality checks for authorization.

**DBAC and Structural Segregation:**
User roles (`Student`/`Teacher`) are stored directly in the `/users/{userId}` document. Security rules will check `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'Teacher'` to determine user capabilities without custom claims, adhering to the DBAC principle. Structural segregation is maintained by ensuring each collection represents a single entity type with consistent access needs. For instance, `notes` and `videos` are separate subcollections even though they both represent class content, as their schemas differ.

This robust structure ensures that security rules are simple, atomic, and easy to debug, avoiding complex `get()` dependencies and supporting efficient, secure querying for all roles and scenarios.
